source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage at MP 153 B

event.top_event: Loss of Load Control - Equipment Drop
actions_taken: Immediate use of wooden boards to cover bent grating
All personnel relocated to platform galley for incident meeting
ESFWP removed from grating using platform crane
Repair materials shipped and received on 04 May 2025
Grating repaired with permanent steel covering
event.summary: On 03 May 2025 at 0830 hours, during a blind lift operation to install an Electric Submersible Fire Water Pump (ESFWP) at MP 153 B platform, a Fiberbond pipe flange contacted an I-beam beneath the top deck. This contact caused the piping to separate at the flange connection, resulting in the ESFWP and 25 feet of attached pipe falling 4 feet to the deck below. The load struck and penetrated steel grating 1-2 feet. Minor damage occurred to deck grating, Fiberbond piping, and the ESFWP. No injuries or pollution resulted from the incident.
recommendations: Provide and discuss more thorough Job Safety Analysis (JSA) to identify hazards during blind lifts
Utilize tag lines to stabilize and control loads during lifting operations
Designate a single source of communication with the Crane Operator during blind lifts
Assign two designated signalmen during blind lifts for full coverage of hazard identification
Ensure only one signalman communicates directly with crane operator to avoid confusion
Implement better communication techniques for efficient and safe operations
Conduct more thorough pre-job evaluation of work areas to identify obstructions and hazards
Keep personnel at safe distance from loads until secured
Issue a Safety Alert regarding this incident

hazards.name: Blind Lift Operations with Limited Visibility

consequences.name: Potential personnel injury from falling load

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and work procedures before operations

supporting_text: Hazards were not properly identified before or during the blind lift and were not discussed with the personnel involved in the operations
GOM Shelf has stated that in the future, they will provide and discuss a more thorough JSA to identify hazards while performing operations including blind lifts

controls.name: Tag Lines for Load Stabilization
controls.barrier_role: Stabilize and control load during lifting to prevent off-center positioning

supporting_text: personnel did not utilize tag lines or any other means of stabilizing the load as it was being lowered into the caisson
Personnel did not utilize tag lines to assist in lowering the lift or raising/stabilizing the lift
Operators were not instructed to utilize tag lines failed to use tag lines or other means necessary to stabilize the lift

controls.name: Signalman Communication Protocol
controls.barrier_role: Provide single, clear communication source to crane operator during blind lifts

supporting_text: Both the Operator with the radio on lower deck, and the Signalman on the deck where the lift was being performed, were giving the Crane Operator instructions at the time of the incident, causing confusion that contributed the incident
Personnel involved in the operation failed to identify a single source of communication from the Signalman to the Crane Operator
Failure to designate one single source of communication to the crane operator during the lift

controls.name: Dual Signalman Coverage for Blind Lifts
controls.barrier_role: Provide full visibility and hazard identification on both sides of lift path
controls.lod_basis: Comprehensive hazard observation during blind operations

supporting_text: While there was a signalman on the top deck in view of the Crane Operator, there was a failure to provide an additional signalman on the lower deck to identify any possible hazards or obstructions on the bottom side of the top deck where the I-beam was located
In the future, more attention should be given to providing methods of keeping personnel away from the lift being made
there will be two designated signalmen during any blind lifts to provide full coverage when identifying hazards

controls.name: Pre-Job Area Evaluation and Hazard Mapping
controls.barrier_role: Identify structural obstructions and hazards in lift path before operations
controls.lod_basis: Thorough site assessment prior to lifting

supporting_text: Proper planning ahead of the work being performed could have prevented the incident from occurring
With proper planning, a more thorough evaluation of the area could have been conducted. This evaluation could have identified the I-beam as a hazard that was near the lift
the flange that was connecting the fiber bond piping was about 1 foot away from an I-beam under the top deck

controls.name: Signalman Hazard Detection and Alert
controls.barrier_role: Detect load contact with obstruction and alert crane operator to stop
controls.lod_basis: Real-time observation and communication during lift

supporting_text: The Signalman noticed that the flange that was connecting the fiber bond piping was about 1 foot away from an I-beam
When the Signalman turned his attention back to the pipe flange, he noticed the flange had become hung up on the I-beam
he tried to signal the Crane Operator to stop lifting the load. Before the lift was able to be stopped, the Fiberbond pipe sections separated at the flange

controls.name: Personnel Proximity Control and Safe Distance
controls.barrier_role: Keep personnel away from suspended load to prevent injury from falling equipment

supporting_text: personnel were using their hands to guide the ESFWP into place and were in very close proximity to the falling equipment
Proper tag line use would have also prevented personnel from having to be so close to the ESFWP as it was being lowered, raised and repositioned
more attention should be given to providing methods of keeping personnel away from the lift being made. Personnel should keep a safe distance from the load until secured

controls.name: Immediate Hazard Mitigation - Grating Coverage
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel from falling through damaged grating opening

supporting_text: The operators immediately used wooden boards to cover the bent grating, preventing an open hole hazard in the working area

controls.name: Stop Work Authority (SWA) Invocation
controls.barrier_role: Halt all operations to prevent further incidents and allow investigation

supporting_text: The crew then called an all stop of operations by utilizing Stop Work Authority (SWA)

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
